article.title: [PART-2] 100+ interviews | 50+ companies | 1 offer
article.content:
I made a post last year:
https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/7176708/100-interviews-50-companies-0-offers-by-dbhmq/

After that post, I interviewed with 7 more companies including Oracle, Twilio, Salesforce, etc., and finally received an offer from Oracle.

I finally joined Oracle, but due to my bad time, I was one among the 30,000 in the massacre done by Oracle.

Below are my experiences post layoff.

| #  | Company           | Role  | Result                                     |
| -- | ----------------- | ----- | ------------------------------------------ |
| 1  | Kotak             | SDE 2 | Rejected in 2nd round (DSA)                  |
| 2  | Kgen              | SDE 2 | Rejected in 1st round (System Design)        |
| 3  | Impact Analytics  | SDE 2 | Rejected in 3rd round (Tech Python)          |
| 4  | Amazon            | SDE 2 | 4 rounds completed(Loop) - rejected because of LPs                  |
| 5  | HealthEdge        | SDE 2 | Rejected in 2nd round (Technical)|
| 6  | Google            | SWE III | Rejected in Screening (DSA)                  |
| 7  | Kotak (Fullstack) | SDE 2 | Rejected in 1st round: React, Node.js                   |
| 8  | Redpin            | SDE 2 | Ghosted after 1st round            |
| 9  | Photon            | SDE 2 | Rejected in 1st round (Technical)                 |
| 10 | Arrise            | SDE 2 | Rejected in 3rd round (Java)      |
| 11 | Uber            | SDE 2 | Rejected in 3rd round (LLD)      |

**Again the same rejection story, I'm planning to leave the Software industry. Its not working for me..**

post.content: At least you are getting a call man, I'm trying from last 2 months, not a single call

This time too, will pass. Stay strong brother.

With a high rejection rate, you should try and reassess what is causing you to bomb interviews so frequently. The fact that you're able to land this many, is a testament to your resume. My guess is you mess up somewhere during the presentation part of actual interviews (presenting yourself, your exp, or your technical depth). Try focusing there and see if that changes something!

Let me know if you'd like to talk sometime: https://forms.gle/DJrgnr9B7Zo6xL6n6

Bro, don’t leave. Most people never even get interview opportunities at companies like Oracle, Google, Amazon, and Uber — you reached there multiple times. That itself shows your capability. You only need one “yes” to change everything. Don’t let multiple rejections make you forget how far you’ve already come. Keep going...

I've been in this stage before. Don't lose your spirit. Multiple rejections can be tiring, but take them as an opportunity to enter a phase of contemplation. Reflect deeply, do a small retrospective, and identify the gaps that need attention. Once you work through them, you'll move into a phase of transcending, where growth, clarity, and progress become evident. Keep going. Good luck!
can you share the interview experience for Arrise ?

Your hardwork and consistency to appear in interviews is a motivation for me. I also face similar issue. I got rejected in interviews after solving 70-80% questions, mainly due to communicatoin skills or I Panic in front of interviewer and fumble alot.
Can we connect once ? I want to discuss it to you.
Ok but how you are bringing up these many interview calls….any tip trick you are making
Even I am thinking of leaving IT. It has become a clown show at this point. Everyone wants me to know everything in software
I am also planning to leave Software industry
I couldn't even get an interview scheduled for the companies you've interviewed for. Any tips on how did you got it in the loop.
Bro where are you currently working and whats the yoe?
